name: Arondight
description: Arondight is the sword of Sir Lancelot, one of the most renowned knights of the Round Table in Arthurian legend. Its name is thought to mean 'powerful light' or 'powerful strike.' Unlike Excalibur, which is more closely associated with Arthur's kingship, Arondight is a symbol of Lancelot's unparalleled martial prowess and knightly virtues. Forged by Wayland the Smith, a legendary figure known for crafting exceptional weapons, Arondight possessed exceptional sharpness and balance, making it a deadly weapon in the hands of a skilled warrior like Lancelot. It was said to glow with a radiant light when drawn, further enhancing its mystique and instilling fear in Lancelot's enemies. The sword played a crucial role in many of Lancelot's heroic deeds and tournaments, solidifying his reputation as one of the greatest knights of all time. However, it is also associated with the tragic love affair between Lancelot and Guinevere, which ultimately contributed to the downfall of Camelot. Arondight, therefore, represents both the pinnacle of knightly excellence and the destructive power of forbidden love. Its legacy continues to inspire tales of chivalry, bravery, and the complexities of human relationships. The blade’s properties are often described as unbreakable and capable of cleaving through any armor. Its light serves as a beacon for justice and righteousness, though its use in the context of Lancelot’s adultery casts a shadow upon its otherwise noble nature. The sword is not just a weapon, but a reflection of Lancelot himself – powerful, skilled, and ultimately flawed.
subtitle: Lancelot's radiant and deadly sword.
currentLocation: Unknown, likely lost after Lancelot's death

keyMyths.title: Lancelot's Tournaments
keyMyths.description: Arondight was instrumental in Lancelot's victories in numerous tournaments, establishing him as the greatest knight. Its unparalleled sharpness and radiant glow gave him an edge over his opponents.

keyMyths.title: Lancelot's Battles
keyMyths.description: Lancelot wielded Arondight in various battles, proving its worth against numerous foes. The sword's unbreakable nature and radiant glow made it a formidable weapon in combat.

sources.author: Sir Thomas Malory
sources.description: A comprehensive compilation of Arthurian legends.
sources.text: A comprehensive compilation of Arthurian legends.

sources.author: Chrétien de Troyes
sources.work: Lancelot, the Knight of the Cart
